retry_reason: Failure occurs when no device with the given name exists. Retrying without any expectation that the device will appear is not meaningful; the caller should handle the NULL return as a permanent absence unless it knows the device may be registered later (e.g., in a race with driver loading).
contract_summary: Get-wrapper: acquires a reference on a backlight_device by name via class_find_device_by_name, which increments the underlying device's kobject refcount. Returns NULL if no matching device is found; the caller must check the return value. On success, the caller owns a reference and must release it with backlight_device_put() when the device is no longer needed. The function does not create or initialize the device; it only looks up an already-registered instance.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to look up a backlight device by name and return it with an acquired reference. The only logic is calling class_find_device_by_name (which increments the kobject refcount) and converting the result. No other business logic is present.

check.reason: The function backlight_device_get_by_name returns a backlight_device pointer obtained via class_find_device_by_name, which acquires a reference. The access path bd.dev.kobj.kref.refcount correctly traces from the return value (backlight_device) through its dev, kobj, kref, to the refcount field. The type chain backlight_device--device--kobject--kref--refcount_t matches. Both path and chain have 5 segments, consistent.
